perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the terms below shall have the following meanings: (a) “Confidential Information” shall mean commercial, corporate, scientific, regulatory and technical information, including, without limitation, data, materials, documents, agreements, lists, financial information, investments, information concerning current or proposed products, technologies, applications, service or methods of operation and/or business plans, customers, suppliers, and any copies or versions of the information disclosed, relating to NEW CONFIDENTIAL RIVER or SHIRE, that NEW RIVER or SHIRE, respectively, regards as its highly valuable trade secrets, confidential information, and proprietary property. Where any Confidential Information is derived from the synthesis in a unique manner, or into a unique concept, of various elements that may be contained in the public domain, no part of the Confidential Information shall be deemed to be in the public domain or to be in the Receiving Party’s possession or to be thereafter acquired by the Receiving Party merely because it embraces information in the public domain or general information that the Receiving Party may thereafter acquire.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the terms below shall have the following meanings: (a) “Confidential Information” shall mean information of commercial value. Where any Confidential Information is derived from the synthesis in a unique manner, or into a unique concept, of various elements that may be contained in the public domain, no part of the Confidential Information shall be deemed to be in the public domain or to be in the Receiving Party’s possession or to be thereafter acquired by the Receiving Party merely because it embraces information in the public domain or general information that the Receiving Party may thereafter acquire.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the detailed description of what constitutes 'Confidential Information', the definition becomes vague. Other clauses refer to specific types of information as Confidential Information. However, this section now provides a broad definition that focuses on commercial value, which may not be applicable for all the examples and types of information which might cause confusion when other mentions of 'Confidential Information' are made through the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees: (a) to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence in accordance with this Agreement; (b) to exercise at least the same care in protecting the other Party’s Confidential Information from disclosure as the Party uses with regard to its own Confidential Information (but in no event less than reasonable care); (c) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties (including, without limitation, any clients, affiliates, independent contractors and consultants) without the prior, written consent of the Disclosing Party except as expressly permitted under Paragraph 4; and (d) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a detailed definition of 'Confidential Information', including specific types of data, materials, and information related to NEW CONFIDENTIAL RIVER or SHIRE. The changed text omits this detailed description and provides a broader definition focusing on 'information of commercial value.' This omission creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information' without specifying that it is limited to only information of commercial value. The changed text undermines the scope and protection intended by the original agreement because the term is now more vague, which is explained in the Perturbation Explanation. Therefore, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees not to remove any materials or tangible items containing any of the other Party’s Confidential Information from the premises of the Disclosing Party without the Disclosing Party’s consent. Each Party agrees to comply with any and all terms and conditions that the Disclosing Party imposes upon approved removal of such materials or items, including, without limitation, that the removed materials or items must be returned by a certain date, and that no copies of the removed materials or items are to be made without the consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees not to remove any materials or tangible items containing any of the other Party’s Confidential Information from the premises of the Disclosing Party without the Disclosing Party’s consent.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause about the terms and conditions for removing Confidential Materials, it creates an internal contradiction. Other clauses still references this section, with it's original full meaning. Now, even though the term is still being reffered, the full meaning isn't clear anymore. This creates an enforcemen ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement, subject to the terms and conditions imposed on the removal of Confidential Information under Paragraph 6, sets forth the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and it may not be modified or amended except by a written agreement executed by the parties hereto; provided, however, (a) each existing confidentiality agreement between some or all of the Parties shall survive the execution of this Agreement and continue in full force and effect in accordance with its stated terms and conditions, and (b) the confidentiality obligations arising under the United States Collaboration Agreement, dated March 31, 2005, by and among Shire LLC, Shire Pharmaceuticals Group PLC, and New River Pharmaceuticals Inc., or under the ROW Territory License Agreement, dated March 31, 2005, by and among Shire Pharmaceuticals Ireland Limited, Shire Pharmaceuticals Group PLC, and New River Pharmaceuticals Inc., shall survive the execution of this Agreement and continue in full force and effect in accordance with the stated terms and conditions of each.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific terms and conditions associated with removing confidential information. The contradicted text explicitly refers to "terms and conditions imposed on the removal of Confidential Information under Paragraph 6". Since Paragraph 6 no longer contains those terms and conditions due to the omission, a direct contradiction exists because the contradicted section relies on something that has been removed, creating ambiguity and potential unenforceability. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given if sent by fax copy as directed below and followed by hard copy, dispatched on the same day (a) by a nationally reputable delivery service, prepaid and addressed as set forth below, or (b) by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, and addressed as follows.
perturbation.changed_text: All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given if sent by email, addressed as set forth below.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the method of delivery of legal notices from a written form to email, a contradiction may arise, as emails aren't necessairly secured nor reliable, especially if being sent to other companies. The email being the only method to delivery notices may lead to a breach of the contract, if someone is in need for a fast solution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If to NEW RIVER: New River Pharmaceuticals Inc. The Governor Tyler 1881 Grove Ave Radford, VA 24141 Attention: Legal Department Fax No.: (540) 633-7939 If to SHIRE: Shire Pharmaceuticals, Inc. 725 Chesterbrook Boulevard Wayne, PA 19087 Attention: Legal Department Fax No.: 484-595-8163 CONFIDENTIAL Page 5 of 7 If to L.E.K.: L.E.K. Consulting LLC 28 State Street 16 th Floor Boston, MA 02109 Attention: Mike Clabault Fax No.: 617-951-9392 Any party may change its address for notices by giving the other party notice of such change in the manner provided above.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that notices must be delivered via fax followed by hard copy, either through a reputable delivery service or certified/registered mail. The changed text alters this requirement, stating that notices should be sent via email. The contradicted text provides fax numbers for each party involved, suggesting that fax is a necessary method for sending notices. By changing the required method to email only, the modified text directly contradicts the contradicted text by removing fax as a viable option, rendering the provided fax numbers useless and creating a strong contradiction regarding the accepted method for delivering legal notices.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended, modified, altered or supplemented except by a writing signed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended, modified, altered or supplemented verbally by both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the way the agreement should be modified (verbally instead of by writing), an internal ambiguity is created, which causes the original terms of the agreement to be broken or ignored.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement, subject to the terms and conditions imposed on the removal of Confidential Information under Paragraph 6, sets forth the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and it may not be modified or amended except by a written agreement executed by the parties hereto; provided, however, (a) each existing confidentiality agreement between some or all of the Parties shall survive the execution of this Agreement and continue in full force and effect in accordance with its stated terms and conditions, and (b) the confidentiality obligations arising under the United States Collaboration Agreement, dated March 31, 2005, by and among Shire LLC, Shire Pharmaceuticals Group PLC, and New River Pharmaceuticals Inc., or under the ROW Territory License Agreement, dated March 31, 2005, by and among Shire Pharmaceuticals Ireland Limited, Shire Pharmaceuticals Group PLC, and New River Pharmaceuticals Inc., shall survive the execution of this Agreement and continue in full force and effect in accordance with the stated terms and conditions of each.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can only be modified in writing. The changed text states that the agreement can be modified verbally. The contradicted text also states that the agreement can only be modified by written agreement. The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by allowing verbal modifications, leading to a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction because the way the agreement should be modified was altered, breaking compatibility with the contradicted text.
